title:	Phase 2: Conversation branching with context lineage
state:	OPEN
author:	marcus-sa
labels:	
comments:	0
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	6
--
## Problem
Conversations drift across topics, and flat threads become too broad to serve as clean provenance sources.

## Goal
Make branching a first-class conversation workflow so users can split sub-problems into focused threads while preserving context lineage.

## Scope
Implement conversation branching and nested branch display.

### Branch Behavior
1. Create a new `conversation` node.
2. Link child to parent via `BRANCHED_FROM` edge.
3. Carry forward relevant parent context entities into the child branch.
4. Bias early extraction/project resolution toward inherited context.

### Data Model
Add edge:
- `BRANCHED_FROM`: `Conversation -> Conversation`
  - `branched_at`
  - `context_entities` (array of record refs)

### UI Behavior
- Sidebar renders branched conversations as nested under the parent.
- Branch action available from the active conversation.
- Branch creation navigates user into the new branch.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can branch from an existing conversation.
- [ ] Branch stores lineage through `BRANCHED_FROM`.
- [ ] Branched conversation starts with inherited context entities.
- [ ] Sidebar displays parent/child branch nesting.
- [ ] Extraction/project resolution in early branch turns uses inherited context bias.

## Out of Scope
- Topic-drift auto-suggestion UX (track separately if needed).
- Auto-archiving lifecycle.
- Graph-powered conversation search UI.
- Inactive conversation summaries.

content.text:
I now have a thorough picture of the entire conversation data model. Here is the complete findings report:

---

## Conversation Data Model - Complete Findings

### 1. SurrealDB Schema (`/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/schema/surreal-schema.surql`)

**`conversation` table (lines 1-12):**
```sql
DEFINE TABLE conversation SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD createdAt        ON conversation TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD updatedAt        ON conversation TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD workspace        ON conversation TYPE record<workspace>;
DEFINE FIELD source           ON conversation TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD timestamp        ON conversation TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE FIELD messages         ON conversation TYPE option<array<record<message>>>;  -- note: not actually used for storage; messages are fetched via WHERE clause
DEFINE FIELD title            ON conversation TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD title_source     ON conversation TYPE option<string> ASSERT $value = NONE OR $value IN ["message", "entity"];
DEFINE FIELD embedding        ON conversation TYPE option<array<float>>;

DEFINE INDEX conversation_createdAt ON conversation FIELDS createdAt;
DEFINE INDEX conversation_workspace ON conversation FIELDS workspace;
```

**`message` table (lines 14-23):**
```sql
DEFINE TABLE message SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD conversation     ON message TYPE record<conversation>;
DEFINE FIELD role             ON message TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["user", "assistant"];
DEFINE FIELD text             ON message TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD createdAt        ON message TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD suggestions      ON message TYPE option<array<string>>;
DEFINE FIELD clientMessageId  ON message TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD embedding        ON message TYPE option<array<float>>;

DEFINE INDEX message_conversation_createdAt ON message FIELDS conversation, createdAt;
DEFINE INDEX idx_message_embedding          ON message FIELDS embedding HNSW DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE;
```

**Key relation edges involving conversations:**
- `touched_by TYPE RELATION IN project OUT conversation` (lines 287-291) — links projects to conversations they were discussed in; carries `first_mention_at` and `entity_count` (derived from extraction, computed during processing)
- `decided_in TYPE RELATION IN decision OUT conversation | meeting` (line 277) — links a decision to the conversation where it was made
- `extraction_relation TYPE RELATION IN message | document_chunk OUT workspace | project | person | feature | task | decision | question` (lines 247-260) — the provenance link from a message to every extracted entity

**SurrealQL stored function for conversations (line 423-430):**
```sql
DEFINE FUNCTION fn::conversation_recent($conversation: record<conversation>, $limit: int) {
  LET $safe_limit = math::min([math::max([$limit, 1]), 50]);
  RETURN SELECT id, role, text, createdAt
  FROM message
  WHERE conversation = $conversation
  ORDER BY createdAt DESC
  LIMIT $safe_limit;
};
```

---

### 2. Conversation Creation

There are two creation paths:

**Path A: Workspace creation** (`/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/workspace/workspace-routes.ts`, `handleCreateWorkspace`):
- Creates the first conversation in a transaction atomically with the workspace and owner person
- Sets `source: "onboarding"`, `title_source: "message"`, `title: null` (no initial title)
- Also creates a starter assistant message in the same transaction

**Path B: Chat message ingress** (`/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/chat/chat-ingress.ts`, `handlePostChatMessage`):
- `conversationId = parsed.data.conversationId ?? randomUUID()` — client may pass an existing ID or leave it empty for a new conversation
- If the conversation does not yet exist: creates it with `title: deriveMessageTitle(messageText)`, `title_source: "message"`, and conditionally `source: "onboarding"` if the workspace hasn't finished onboarding
- If it exists: validates workspace scope ownership (`existingConversation.workspace.id === workspaceRecord.id`), then just updates `updatedAt`
- Immediately creates the user message record in the same transaction

**Title lifecycle** (`/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/workspace/conversation-sidebar.ts`):
- Initial title: first message text, truncated to 72 chars (`deriveMessageTitle`)
- Upgrade: `maybeUpgradeConversationTitle()` runs after every extraction; if `title_source === "message"` and ≥3 non-person/non-workspace entities extracted, upgrades to dominant project name (or most-common entity text), and sets `title_source = "entity"` (entity titles never re-upgraded)

---

### 3. Conversation Context Building (`/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/chat/context.ts`)

**`buildChatContext()`** fetches five things in parallel:
1. `listConversationEntities` — extraction_relation edges for the current conversation (up to 60), giving entities with kind, name, confidence
2. `listWorkspaceProjectSummaries` — up to 20 projects with active task counts
3. `listWorkspaceRecentDecisions` — up to 12 recent decisions
4. `listWorkspaceOpenQuestions` — up to 12 open questions
5. `listWorkspaceOpenObservations` — up to 10 open observations

**`buildSystemPrompt()`** assembles these into the orchestrator system prompt as formatted text blocks: entities from this conversation, projects, decisions, questions, and active observations. It then lists all 12 orchestrator tools and subagent instructions.

**Extraction context loading** (`/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts`):
- `loadAssistantConversationContext()` — uses `fn::conversation_recent` with limit 10, reversed for chronological order (sent to the orchestrator as prior turn history)
- `loadExtractionConversationContext()` — uses `fn::conversation_recent` with limit 31, identifies the current message, provides up to 30 prior messages as extraction history
- `loadConversationGraphContext()` — queries `extraction_relation` for all entities extracted from this conversation's messages (up to 60), deduped by entity ID, with their text loaded for extraction grounding

---

### 4. Message Model and Conversation Relationship

Messages link back to their conversation via a direct `conversation` field (a `record<conversation>` reference — no array stored on the conversation itself despite the schema field). Key facts:

- Messages are always fetched with `WHERE conversation = $conversationRecord ORDER BY createdAt ASC/DESC`
- A message carries: `role` ("user"|"assistant"), `text`, `createdAt`, optional `suggestions` (array of string prompt suggestions shown in UI), optional `clientMessageId` (for dedup/idempotency), and optional `embedding`
- The user message `RecordId` is a random UUID assigned at ingress; the assistant message ID is the SSE `messageId` (also a random UUID) — used as the SSE stream key
- The `extraction_relation` edge links `message → entity` and stores `confidence`, `extracted_at`, `from_text`, `evidence`, `evidence_source`, `resolved_from`
- The `message` HNSW index enables semantic search via `searchConversationMessagesByEmbedding()` in the `get_conversation_history` orchestrator tool

**`ConversationRow` TypeScript type** (`/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/extraction/types.ts`):
```typescript
export type ConversationRow = {
  id: RecordId<"conversation", string>;
  createdAt: Date | string;
  updatedAt: Date | string;
  workspace: RecordId<"workspace", string>;
  source?: string;          // "onboarding" for onboarding conversations
  title?: string;
  title_source?: "message" | "entity";
};
```

---

### 5. Branching and Threading - Current State

There is **zero implemented branching** in the codebase. No `BRANCHED_FROM` table, edge, or field exists anywhere in:
- `schema/surreal-schema.surql`
- Any server TypeScript file
- Any query
- Any type definition

The grep search for `BRANCHED_FROM`, `branched_from`, `branchOf`, `branch_of`, `branchFrom`, `parent_conversation`, `parentConversation` returned only hits in `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/README.md`.

**What the README says about branching** (it is a planned Phase 2 feature):
- `BRANCHED_FROM` is described as a `Conversation → Conversation` edge with fields `branched_at` and `context_entities[]`
- The intended flow: user selects a message range or clicks "branch from here" → creates a new Conversation node with a `BRANCHED_FROM` edge to the parent → new conversation carries forward relevant graph context (parent entities pre-loaded for high-confidence project/feature resolution) → branched conversations display nested under parent in sidebar
- The system is also planned to detect conversation drift (extraction pipeline notices recent messages producing entities from a different project cluster) and suggest branching via a non-intrusive suggestion pill
- README phases: Phase 1 = current (grouping by `TOUCHED_BY`, auto-titles, new conversation button); Phase 2 = branching + nested sidebar; Phase 3 = auto-archiving + graph-powered search + conversation summaries

---

### Summary Map

| Concern | File | Key Detail |
|---------|------|------------|
| Schema: conversation + message | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/schema/surreal-schema.surql` lines 1-23 | `message.conversation → record<conversation>`, HNSW on message.embedding |
| Schema: graph edges | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/schema/surreal-schema.surql` lines 227-305 | `touched_by`, `decided_in`, `extraction_relation` are the relevant edges |
| TypeScript types | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/extraction/types.ts` | `ConversationRow`, `MessageContextRow`, `ExtractionConversationContext` |
| Conversation creation (workspace) | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/workspace/workspace-routes.ts` lines 84-141 | Atomic transaction, `source: "onboarding"` |
| Conversation creation (chat) | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/chat/chat-ingress.ts` lines 41-118 | New or resume, workspace scope check |
| Chat processing | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/chat/chat-processor.ts` | Extraction → persistence → orchestrator → SSE |
| Context building | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/chat/context.ts` | `buildChatContext()` + `buildSystemPrompt()` |
| Context loaders | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts` | `loadAssistantConversationContext()`, `loadExtractionConversationContext()`, `loadConversationGraphContext()` |
| Sidebar / title / touched_by | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/workspace/conversation-sidebar.ts` | Title upgrade logic, `refreshConversationTouchedBy()` |
| Tool: history search | `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/boston/app/src/server/chat/tools/get-conversation-history.ts` | Semantic search across messages, returns conversationId + excerpts |
| Branching | README only | Planned Phase 2 — no implementation exists |

## Extraction and Context Resolution: Complete Data Flow

### 1. Chat Ingress (`chat/chat-ingress.ts`)

The ingress layer is intentionally thin and synchronous with the HTTP response:

1. Parses and validates the incoming request (workspace ID, conversation ID, message text, optional attachment, onboarding action).
2. Resolves the workspace record, validates it exists and scope matches.
3. In a single transaction:
   - Creates or updates the `conversation` record (workspace-scoped, derives title from the message text on first create).
   - Creates the `message` record (role `user`, linked to the conversation).
   - Increments `onboarding_turn_count` if still onboarding.
4. Registers an SSE stream slot for the `messageId`.
5. Fires `processChatMessage()` as a detached async task (`void`).
6. Immediately returns a `ChatMessageResponse` with the stream URL — the client polls SSE for progress.

---

### 2. Chat Processor (`chat/chat-processor.ts`)

This is the async orchestration layer. It runs after the HTTP response is already sent:

**Step A — Load context (parallel)**

Three context loads fire before any extraction:

- `loadAssistantConversationContext()` — fetches the last 10 messages (reversed to chronological order) via `fn::conversation_recent`. Used later as the LLM's message history.
- `loadExtractionConversationContext()` — fetches the last 31 messages via the same SurrealDB function, separates the current message from history (up to 30 history messages). Feeds the extraction model with conversation history for reference resolution.
- `loadConversationGraphContext()` — fetches up to 60 entities previously extracted from this conversation (via `extraction_relation` edges), reading their current text from the graph. Feeds the extraction model's "existing graph context" section.

**Step B — Optional attachment ingestion**

If an attachment exists, `ingestAttachment()` chunks the document and runs extraction per chunk, streaming `extraction` and `onboarding_seed` SSE events as each chunk completes. Collected results accumulate into `persistedEntities`, `persistedRelationships`, `embeddingTargets`, `extractedTools`, `unresolvedAssigneeNames`.

**Step C — Text extraction**

`extractStructuredGraph()` calls the LLM (Haiku) with:
- Conversation history (for reference resolution only)
- The current message (the extraction source)
- `resolvedFromConstraints` — a strict allowlist of history message IDs that the LLM may use for `resolvedFromMessageId`
- Existing graph context (entities already extracted from this conversation)
- Workspace scope (workspace name, existing project names)

The LLM produces `{ entities, relationships, tools }` in structured JSON (Zod-validated).

**Step D — Persist extraction**

`persistExtractionOutput()` processes each extracted entity:
1. Post-validates (filters below `extractionStoreThreshold`).
2. Normalizes relationship kinds.
3. For each entity, calls `upsertGraphEntity()` (see section 3 below).
4. For each relationship, creates an `entity_relation` RELATE edge with `kind`, `confidence`, source message, and evidence text.
5. Resolves assignee names to workspace persons via `findWorkspacePersonByName`, writing `owner`/`owner_name` or `decided_by`/`decided_by_name` back onto the entity.

**Step E — Onboarding state transition**

`transitionOnboardingState()` determines whether to stay in onboarding flow or mark it complete.

**Step F — Response generation**

Two paths:
- **Onboarding active**: `generateOnboardingAssistantReply()` — a lighter model call.
- **Onboarding complete**: `runOrchestrator()` — the full graph-aware orchestrator (see section 5).

**Step G — SSE events emitted**

`extraction`, `onboarding_seed`, `onboarding_state`, `token`, `assistant_message`, `done|error` — all streaming back to the client.

---

### 3. Entity Upsert and Deduplication (`extraction/entity-upsert.ts`)

The core of how context accumulates across conversations:

Each extracted entity goes through a three-stage dedup before it ever gets created:

**Stage 1 — Exact name match**
All existing workspace-scoped entities of the same kind are fetched from the DB. Exact normalized text match (`normalizeName`) triggers a merge: only a new provenance edge (`extraction_relation`) is created, no new record. If the incoming text is richer, the entity's canonical name is upgraded (`maybeUpgradeMergedEntityName`).

**Stage 2 — Embedding similarity + fuzzy name match**
If no exact match, cosine similarity is computed against all candidates. If `similarity > 0.95` AND `isFuzzyNameMatch()` passes — treat as same entity, create provenance edge only.

**Stage 3 — New entity**
Create a new record with the entity-specific field layout:
- `project` / `feature`: `name`, `status: active`
- `task`: `title`, `status: open`, `extraction_confidence`, `source_message`
- `decision`: `summary`, `status: extracted`, `extraction_confidence`
- `question`: `text`, `status: open`, `extraction_confidence`

All new entities also get an `embedding` (created inline via `createEmbedding` before the candidate search).

**Possible duplicate link**
If similarity is in the 0.80–0.95 range, a `POSSIBLE_DUPLICATE` `entity_relation` edge is created alongside the new record.

**Project/feature graph scoping**
After creation:
- `project`: a `has_project` edge is created linking `workspace → project`. The in-memory `workspaceProjects` array is updated immediately so subsequent entities in the same extraction batch see it.
- `feature`: `resolveEntityProject()` attempts to match an existing project by name from the prompt text; if found, a `has_feature` edge is created.
- `task`, `decision`, `question`: same project resolution, then a `belongs_to` RELATE edge.

**Provenance edge**
Every entity (new or merged) gets an `extraction_relation` edge from the source (`message` or `document_chunk`) to the entity record, with `confidence`, `model`, `from_text`, `evidence`, `evidence_source`, and optionally `resolved_from` (pointing to the earlier message the LLM resolved a reference from).

---

### 4. Conversation Graph Context (`extraction/context-loaders.ts`)

`loadConversationGraphContext()` is how the extraction model is told what has already been extracted in this conversation. It queries:

```sql
SELECT `in`, out, confidence, extracted_at
FROM extraction_relation
WHERE `in` IN (SELECT VALUE id FROM message WHERE conversation = $conversation)
ORDER BY extracted_at DESC
LIMIT $limit
```

This gives the most recent 60 provenance edges from messages in this conversation to entities. After deduplication by entity ID (keeps highest-confidence one), each entity's display text is fetched via `readEntityText()` and formatted as:

```
[entity:task:<uuid>] task: Implement auth flow (confidence 0.92, source message <uuid>)
```

This section is injected into the extraction prompt as "Existing graph context". Its purpose is to let the LLM recognize that "it" or "that" refers to an already-extracted entity and produce a `resolvedFromMessageId` instead of extracting a duplicate.

---

### 5. Chat Context for the Orchestrator (`chat/context.ts`)

After extraction is done, `runOrchestrator()` calls `buildChatContext()`, which loads the orchestrator's system prompt context in parallel:

| Loader | Query | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `listConversationEntities()` | `extraction_relation` where message in conversation, up to 60 | Entities extracted from **this conversation** (workspace-scoped, deduplicated) |
| `listWorkspaceProjectSummaries()` | `has_project` from workspace, active task counts | Workspace-wide project list |
| `listWorkspaceRecentDecisions()` | `belongs_to` + workspace message scope, last 12 | Recent decisions across all workspace conversations |
| `listWorkspaceOpenQuestions()` | `belongs_to` + workspace message scope, status=open, last 12 | Open questions across workspace |
| `listWorkspaceOpenObservations()` | `observation` table, status=open, workspace-scoped, last 10 | Agent-created observations (risks/conflicts) |

`listConversationEntities()` specifically walks `extraction_relation` edges (same query as `loadConversationGraphContext` but via `graph/queries.ts` with an additional workspace scope check via `isEntityInWorkspace()`), then resolves each entity's canonical name from its table.

The assembled `ChatContext` is serialized into the orchestrator's system prompt as human-readable bullet lists under sections: "This Conversation" (conversation-scoped entities), "Workspace Overview" (projects, decisions, questions), and "Active Observations".

---

### 6. Project Resolution Logic (`workspace/workspace-scope.ts`)

`resolveEntityProject()` determines which project to link a new entity to:
- 0 projects in workspace: no link.
- 1 project: always link to it.
- 2+ projects: search the prompt text + entity text for a word-boundary match of a project name; only link if exactly one project matches.

This is called for `feature`, `task`, `decision`, and `question` entities at creation time.

---

### 7. No "Context Entity Inheritance" — Dedup IS the Mechanism

There is no explicit "context entity inheritance" pattern. Context accumulates through two complementary mechanisms:

1. **Entity deduplication**: Subsequent messages that reference the same entity (by name or semantically) resolve to the same DB record. The entity record itself accumulates `extraction_relation` edges from every message that mentioned it. The provenance chain (`resolvedFromMessageId`) tracks cross-turn reference resolution back to the originating message.

2. **Graph context injection**: Every extraction call receives the prior entities extracted from this conversation as a "semantic index." This tells the extraction model what has already been extracted, preventing re-extraction and enabling pronoun/reference resolution (`resolvedFromMessageId`).

The conversation is not the unit of entity ownership — the **workspace** is. Entities extracted from any conversation in the workspace are candidates for deduplication against entities from any other conversation. A `task` or `decision` scoped to a project via `belongs_to` is visible workspace-wide to the orchestrator, not just from the conversation that first mentioned it.

Design an implementation plan for conversation branching with context lineage (GitHub issue #6). Here is everything I've learned from exploring the codebase:

## Issue Requirements

**Goal:** Make branching a first-class conversation workflow so users can split sub-problems into focused threads while preserving context lineage.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- User can branch from an existing conversation
- Branch stores lineage through `BRANCHED_FROM` edge
- Branched conversation starts with inherited context entities
- Sidebar displays parent/child branch nesting
- Extraction/project resolution in early branch turns uses inherited context bias

## Current Architecture

### Data Model (schema/surreal-schema.surql)
- `conversation` table: SCHEMAFULL with fields: createdAt, updatedAt, workspace (record<workspace>), source (option<string>), timestamp (option<datetime>), messages (option<array<record<message>>>), title (option<string>), title_source (option<string>), embedding (option<array<float>>)
- `message` table: conversation (record<conversation>), role, text, createdAt, suggestions, clientMessageId, embedding
- `touched_by` TYPE RELATION IN project OUT conversation — links projects to conversations
- `extraction_relation` TYPE RELATION IN message|document_chunk OUT entities — provenance

No branching exists anywhere. The README describes the plan but no code implements it.

### Conversation Creation
Two paths:
1. **Workspace creation** (workspace-routes.ts): Creates first conversation in atomic transaction
2. **Chat ingress** (chat/chat-ingress.ts): `conversationId = parsed.data.conversationId ?? randomUUID()`. Creates conversation on first message with title from message text.

### Context Loading (extraction/context-loaders.ts)
- `loadAssistantConversationContext()` — last 10 messages via `fn::conversation_recent`
- `loadExtractionConversationContext()` — last 31 messages for extraction history
- `loadConversationGraphContext()` — up to 60 entities via extraction_relation edges from messages in the conversation

### Chat Context (chat/context.ts)
- `buildChatContext()` loads: conversation entities, workspace projects, recent decisions, open questions, open observations
- `listConversationEntities` — extraction_relation edges for current conversation
- `buildSystemPrompt()` — assembles orchestrator system prompt with entities and tools

### Chat Processing (chat/chat-processor.ts)
1. Load assistant context + extraction context + graph context + workspace projects
2. Optional attachment ingestion
3. Text extraction (`extractStructuredGraph`)
4. Persist extraction output
5. Refresh touched_by edges + maybe upgrade title
6. Run orchestrator or onboarding reply
7. Emit SSE events

### Sidebar (workspace/conversation-sidebar.ts)
- `buildWorkspaceConversationSidebar()`: loads all conversations for workspace, loads touched_by edges, groups by dominant project using `groupConversationsByProject()`
- Returns `{ groups: ProjectConversationGroup[], unlinked: ConversationSidebarItem[] }`
- `ConversationSidebarItem = { id, title, updatedAt }`

### UI (client/routes/chat-page.tsx)
- Sidebar renders project groups with nested conversation buttons
- `onNewConversation()`: resets state (no API call until first message)
- `onSelectConversation()`: calls `loadConversation()` which fetches `/api/workspaces/:id/conversations/:id`
- `onSendMessage()`: generates clientMessageId UUID, sends to `/api/chat/messages`, streams response via SSE
- No per-conversation URL routes — all state is in-memory

### Shared Contracts (shared/contracts.ts)
- `ChatMessageRequest = { clientMessageId, workspaceId, conversationId?, text, onboardingAction? }`
- `ConversationSidebarItem = { id, title, updatedAt }`
- `ProjectConversationGroup = { projectId, projectName, conversations: ConversationSidebarItem[], featureActivity }`
- `WorkspaceConversationSidebarResponse = { groups, unlinked }`

### API Routes (runtime/start-server.ts)
- POST /api/workspaces — create workspace
- GET /api/workspaces/:id/bootstrap — load workspace + conversation + sidebar
- GET /api/workspaces/:id/sidebar — refresh sidebar
- GET /api/workspaces/:id/conversations/:id — load conversation messages
- POST /api/chat/messages — send message
- GET /api/chat/stream/:messageId — SSE stream

## Design Considerations

1. **BRANCHED_FROM edge**: The issue specifies a `BRANCHED_FROM` edge from Conversation → Conversation with `branched_at` and `context_entities[]` (array of record refs). Per AGENTS.md rules:
   - TYPE RELATION tables need `RELATE` not `CREATE`
   - SCHEMAFULL requires all nested fields declared
   - Must use `RecordId` objects everywhere

2. **Context entity inheritance**: When branching, the `context_entities` array on the edge stores which entities from the parent should carry forward. These should be loaded as additional context for the new conversation's extraction pipeline.

3. **Extraction bias**: The extraction pipeline should see inherited entities in `loadConversationGraphContext()` so early turns in the branch have context without needing to re-extract.

4. **Sidebar nesting**: `ConversationSidebarItem` needs a `children` field. The sidebar builder must query `BRANCHED_FROM` edges and build a tree structure. The UI needs recursive rendering.

5. **Branch creation**: Needs a new API endpoint (e.g., `POST /api/workspaces/:id/conversations/:parentId/branch`). This creates a new conversation, RELATE via BRANCHED_FROM, copy context_entities, and return the new conversation ID.

6. **UI branch action**: The active conversation needs a "Branch" button that triggers branch creation and navigates into the new branch.

Please design a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan covering all layers (schema, server, contracts, UI). Consider the existing patterns and conventions in the codebase (AGENTS.md rules about null, RecordId, SCHEMAFULL, etc.).

Design an implementation plan for conversation branching with context lineage (GitHub issue #6). Here is everything I've learned from exploring the codebase:

## Issue Requirements

**Goal:** Make branching a first-class conversation workflow so users can split sub-problems into focused threads while preserving context lineage.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- User can branch from an existing conversation
- Branch stores lineage through `BRANCHED_FROM` edge
- Branched conversation starts with inherited context entities
- Sidebar displays parent/child branch nesting
- Extraction/project resolution in early branch turns uses inherited context bias

## Current Architecture

### Data Model (schema/surreal-schema.surql)
- `conversation` table: SCHEMAFULL with fields: createdAt, updatedAt, workspace (record<workspace>), source (option<string>), timestamp (option<datetime>), messages (option<array<record<message>>>), title (option<string>), title_source (option<string>), embedding (option<array<float>>)
- `message` table: conversation (record<conversation>), role, text, createdAt, suggestions, clientMessageId, embedding
- `touched_by` TYPE RELATION IN project OUT conversation — links projects to conversations
- `extraction_relation` TYPE RELATION IN message|document_chunk OUT entities — provenance

No branching exists anywhere. The README describes the plan but no code implements it.

### Conversation Creation
Two paths:
1. **Workspace creation** (workspace-routes.ts): Creates first conversation in atomic transaction
2. **Chat ingress** (chat/chat-ingress.ts): `conversationId = parsed.data.conversationId ?? randomUUID()`. Creates conversation on first message with title from message text.

### Context Loading (extraction/context-loaders.ts)
- `loadAssistantConversationContext()` — last 10 messages via `fn::conversation_recent`
- `loadExtractionConversationContext()` — last 31 messages for extraction history
- `loadConversationGraphContext()` — up to 60 entities via extraction_relation edges from messages in the conversation

### Chat Context (chat/context.ts)
- `buildChatContext()` loads: conversation entities, workspace projects, recent decisions, open questions, open observations
- `listConversationEntities` — extraction_relation edges for current conversation
- `buildSystemPrompt()` — assembles orchestrator system prompt with entities and tools

### Chat Processing (chat/chat-processor.ts)
1. Load assistant context + extraction context + graph context + workspace projects
2. Optional attachment ingestion
3. Text extraction (`extractStructuredGraph`)
4. Persist extraction output
5. Refresh touched_by edges + maybe upgrade title
6. Run orchestrator or onboarding reply
7. Emit SSE events

### Sidebar (workspace/conversation-sidebar.ts)
- `buildWorkspaceConversationSidebar()`: loads all conversations for workspace, loads touched_by edges, groups by dominant project using `groupConversationsByProject()`
- Returns `{ groups: ProjectConversationGroup[], unlinked: ConversationSidebarItem[] }`
- `ConversationSidebarItem = { id, title, updatedAt }`

### UI (client/routes/chat-page.tsx)
- Sidebar renders project groups with nested conversation buttons
- `onNewConversation()`: resets state (no API call until first message)
- `onSelectConversation()`: calls `loadConversation()` which fetches `/api/workspaces/:id/conversations/:id`
- `onSendMessage()`: generates clientMessageId UUID, sends to `/api/chat/messages`, streams response via SSE
- No per-conversation URL routes — all state is in-memory

### Shared Contracts (shared/contracts.ts)
- `ChatMessageRequest = { clientMessageId, workspaceId, conversationId?, text, onboardingAction? }`
- `ConversationSidebarItem = { id, title, updatedAt }`
- `ProjectConversationGroup = { projectId, projectName, conversations: ConversationSidebarItem[], featureActivity }`
- `WorkspaceConversationSidebarResponse = { groups, unlinked }`

### API Routes (runtime/start-server.ts)
- POST /api/workspaces — create workspace
- GET /api/workspaces/:id/bootstrap — load workspace + conversation + sidebar
- GET /api/workspaces/:id/sidebar — refresh sidebar
- GET /api/workspaces/:id/conversations/:id — load conversation messages
- POST /api/chat/messages — send message
- GET /api/chat/stream/:messageId — SSE stream

## Design Considerations

1. **BRANCHED_FROM edge**: The issue specifies a `BRANCHED_FROM` edge from Conversation → Conversation with `branched_at` and `context_entities[]` (array of record refs). Per AGENTS.md rules:
   - TYPE RELATION tables need `RELATE` not `CREATE`
   - SCHEMAFULL requires all nested fields declared
   - Must use `RecordId` objects everywhere

2. **Context entity inheritance**: When branching, the `context_entities` array on the edge stores which entities from the parent should carry forward. These should be loaded as additional context for the new conversation's extraction pipeline.

3. **Extraction bias**: The extraction pipeline should see inherited entities in `loadConversationGraphContext()` so early turns in the branch have context without needing to re-extract.

4. **Sidebar nesting**: `ConversationSidebarItem` needs a `children` field. The sidebar builder must query `BRANCHED_FROM` edges and build a tree structure. The UI needs recursive rendering.

5. **Branch creation**: Needs a new API endpoint (e.g., `POST /api/workspaces/:id/conversations/:parentId/branch`). This creates a new conversation, RELATE via BRANCHED_FROM, copy context_entities, and return the new conversation ID.

6. **UI branch action**: The active conversation needs a "Branch" button that triggers branch creation and navigates into the new branch.

Please design a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan covering all layers (schema, server, contracts, UI). Consider the existing patterns and conventions in the codebase (AGENTS.md rules about null, RecordId, SCHEMAFULL, etc.).

# Plan: Conversation Branching with Context Lineage (Issue #6)

## Context

Conversations drift across topics, and flat threads become too broad to serve as clean provenance sources. This implements branching as a first-class workflow — users can split sub-problems into focused threads while preserving context lineage. A `branched_from` relation edge stores the lineage, inherited context entities bias early extraction, and the sidebar renders branches nested under their parents.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1 — Schema: `branched_from` relation table

**File:** `schema/surreal-schema.surql`

Add after the existing `superseded_by` table (~line 306):

```sql
DEFINE TABLE branched_from TYPE RELATION IN conversation OUT conversation SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD branched_at ON branched_from TYPE datetime;
DEFINE FIELD context_entities ON branched_from TYPE array<record>;
DEFINE FIELD context_entities[*] ON branched_from TYPE record;
DEFINE INDEX branched_from_out ON branched_from FIELDS out;
DEFINE INDEX branched_from_in ON branched_from FIELDS `in`;
```

- `in` = the new branch conversation, `out` = the parent ("branch BRANCHED_FROM parent")
- `context_entities` holds polymorphic `record` refs (task, decision, feature, project, etc.)
- Index on `out` enables efficient "find children of parent" sidebar queries
- Index on `in` enables efficient "find parent of branch" context loading

---

### Step 2 — Shared contracts

**File:** `app/src/shared/contracts.ts`

Add branch request/response types:

```typescript
export type BranchConversationRequest = {
  contextEntityIds?: string[];
};

export type BranchConversationResponse = {
  conversationId: string;
  parentConversationId: string;
};
```

Extend `ConversationSidebarItem` with optional branch nesting:

```typescript
export type ConversationSidebarItem = {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  updatedAt: string;
  parentId?: string;           // set on branch items
  branches?: ConversationSidebarItem[];  // nested children
};
```

Both new fields are optional — existing consumers unaffected.

---

### Step 3 — Branch creation endpoint

**New file:** `app/src/server/chat/branch-conversation.ts`

Handler: `createBranchConversationHandler(deps) -> (workspaceId, parentConversationId, request) => Response`

Logic:
1. Validate request body with zod (`contextEntityIds: z.array(z.string()).default([])`)
2. Resolve workspace via `resolveWorkspaceRecord()` — fail fast if missing
3. Select parent conversation, validate workspace scope match
4. If `contextEntityIds` is empty, auto-populate from parent's `extraction_relation` edges using `loadConversationGraphContext(surreal, parentConversationId, 30)` — smart default that inherits parent's active entities
5. Parse entity ID strings via `parseRecordIdString()` from `graph/queries.ts` (allows task, decision, question, feature, project, person tables)
6. Single transaction: CREATE new conversation + RELATE `branched_from` edge with `branched_at` and `context_entities`
7. Branch title: `"Branch of: {parentTitle}"` (truncated to 60 chars), `title_source: "message"` so it can be upgraded by extraction later
8. Return `{ conversationId, parentConversationId }` with 201 status

**File:** `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts`

Register route:
```
POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/conversations/:parentId/branch
```

Follow the `createWorkItemAcceptHandler` standalone pattern.

---

### Step 4 — Sidebar branch nesting

**File:** `app/src/server/workspace/conversation-sidebar.ts`

Modify `buildWorkspaceConversationSidebar()`:

1. After loading conversations and touched_by edges, query `branched_from` edges:
   ```sql
   SELECT `in`, out FROM branched_from WHERE `in` IN $conversations;
   ```
2. Build `parentMap` (childId -> parentId) and `childrenMap` (parentId -> childIds[])
3. Add `parentId` to `ConversationGroupInput` type
4. In `groupConversationsByProject()`, skip conversations that have a `parentId` (they're branches, not top-level)
5. After grouping, attach branches recursively to their parent `ConversationSidebarItem` via a helper:
   ```typescript
   function attachBranches(item, allItems, childrenMap) -> item with branches populated
   ```
6. Same recursive attachment applies to unlinked conversations

---

### Step 5 — Context inheritance for extraction

**File:** `app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts`

Extend `loadConversationGraphContext()` signature:
```typescript
loadConversationGraphContext(surreal, conversationId, limit, options?: { inheritedEntityIds?: RecordId[] })
```

When `inheritedEntityIds` is provided and the conversation's own extraction rows are sparse (< 5 entities), load inherited entities directly via `readEntityText()` and append them with confidence 0.7 (marking them as inherited, not directly extracted). Skip any already present in the conversation's own results.

**File:** `app/src/server/chat/chat-processor.ts`

After resolving the conversation record, query for `branched_from` edge:
```sql
SELECT `in`, out, context_entities FROM branched_from WHERE `in` = $conversation LIMIT 1;
```

Pass the `context_entities` as `inheritedEntityIds` to `loadConversationGraphContext()`. This only fires for branch conversations — existing conversations have no edges and are unaffected.

**File:** `app/src/server/chat/context.ts`

Similarly pass inherited entity IDs to `listConversationEntities()` so the orchestrator system prompt shows inherited entities under "Entities already extracted from this conversation" on the first branch message.

---

### Step 6 — UI: branch button and nested sidebar

**File:** `app/src/client/routes/chat-page.tsx`

**Branch action:**
- Add `branchingFromId` state
- Add `onBranchConversation()` function: POST to branch endpoint, then `loadConversation()` into the new branch, then `refreshSidebar()`
- Render "Branch" button in `SessionMessagesHeader` area (only when onboarding is complete and a conversation is active)

**Nested sidebar rendering:**
- Extract a recursive `renderConversationItem()` function that handles depth-based indentation and renders `conv.branches` as a nested `<ul>`
- Add visual indicator for branch items (e.g., `"↳ "` prefix, indent via `paddingLeft`)
- Apply `sidebar-conversation-item--branch` CSS class for branch styling
- Replace the flat `group.conversations.map(...)` and `sidebar.unlinked.map(...)` with calls to the recursive renderer

---

## Critical Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `schema/surreal-schema.surql` | Add `branched_from` TYPE RELATION table |
| `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` | Add branch request/response types, extend sidebar item |
| `app/src/server/chat/branch-conversation.ts` | **New** — branch creation handler |
| `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` | Register POST branch route |
| `app/src/server/workspace/conversation-sidebar.ts` | Query branched_from edges, build nested tree |
| `app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts` | Accept inherited entity IDs, merge into context |
| `app/src/server/chat/chat-processor.ts` | Detect branch, load inherited entity IDs |
| `app/src/server/chat/context.ts` | Pass inherited IDs to orchestrator context |
| `app/src/client/routes/chat-page.tsx` | Branch button, recursive sidebar rendering |

Existing utilities to reuse:
- `parseRecordIdString()` from `app/src/server/graph/queries.ts`
- `loadConversationGraphContext()` from `app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts`
- `resolveWorkspaceRecord()` from `app/src/server/workspace/workspace-scope.ts`
- `readEntityText()` from `app/src/server/extraction/entity-text.ts`
- `jsonResponse()` / `jsonError()` from `app/src/server/http/response.ts`

## Verification

1. **Schema**: Apply schema to test DB, verify with `INFO FOR TABLE branched_from;`
2. **Branch creation**: POST to branch endpoint, verify new conversation + branched_from edge created in SurrealDB
3. **Context inheritance**: Send first message in a branch, verify extraction system prompt contains inherited entities from parent
4. **Sidebar nesting**: GET sidebar, verify branch conversations appear nested under parent with correct structure
5. **UI flow**: Click Branch button -> navigates to new conversation -> sidebar shows nested branch -> first message in branch has context from parent
6. **Existing conversations**: Verify no regressions — conversations without branches render identically

String: export async function listConversationEntities(input: {
  surreal: Surreal;
  conversationRecord: RecordId<"conversation", string>;
  workspaceRecord: RecordId<"workspace", string>;
  limit: number;
}): Promise<ConversationEntity[]> {
  const [rows] = await input.surreal
    .query<[Array<{ in: RecordId<"message", string>; out: GraphEntityRecord; confidence: number; extracted_at: string | Date }>]>(
      [
        "SELECT `in`, out, confidence, extracted_at",
        "FROM extraction_relation",
        "WHERE `in` IN (SELECT VALUE id FROM message WHERE conversation = $conversation)",
        "ORDER BY extracted_at DESC",
        "LIMIT $limit;",
      ].join(" "),
      {
        conversation: input.conversationRecord,
        limit: input.limit,
      },
    )
    .collect<[Array<{ in: RecordId<"message", string>; out: GraphEntityRecord; confidence: number; extracted_at: string | Date }>]>();

  const items: ConversationEntity[] = [];
  const seen = new Set<string>();

  for (const row of rows) {
    const table = row.out.table.name as GraphEntityTable;
    if (
      table !== "workspace" &&
      table !== "project" &&
      table !== "person" &&
      table !== "feature" &&
      table !== "task" &&
      table !== "decision" &&
      table !== "question"
    ) {
      continue;
    }

    const scoped = await isEntityInWorkspace(input.surreal, input.workspaceRecord, row.out);
    if (!scoped) {
      continue;
    }

    const entityKey = `${table}:${toRecordIdString(row.out)}`;
    if (seen.has(entityKey)) {
      continue;
    }

    const name = await readEntityName(input.surreal, row.out);
    if (!name) {
      continue;
    }

    items.push({
      id: toRecordIdString(row.out),
      kind: table,
      name,
      confidence: row.confidence,
      sourceMessageId: toRecordIdString(row.in),
    });
    seen.add(entityKey);
  }

  return items;
}</tool_use_error>

Everything looks clean. Here's a summary of all changes:

## Implementation Complete

### Files changed (12 files, +444/-37 lines):

**Schema:**
- `schema/surreal-schema.surql` — Added `branched_from` TYPE RELATION table with `branched_at`, `context_entities` fields and indexes

**Shared types:**
- `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` — Added `BranchConversationRequest`, `BranchConversationResponse` types; extended `ConversationSidebarItem` with optional `parentId` and `branches`

**New endpoint:**
- `app/src/server/chat/branch-conversation.ts` — **New file** — Branch creation handler with auto-context from parent, workspace scope validation, transactional conversation + edge creation
- `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` — Registered `POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/conversations/:parentId/branch`

**Sidebar nesting:**
- `app/src/server/workspace/conversation-sidebar.ts` — Queries `branched_from` edges, builds parent/child maps, filters branches from top-level grouping, recursively attaches branches to parent items via `attachBranches()`

**Context inheritance:**
- `app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts` — `loadConversationGraphContext` now accepts optional `inheritedEntityIds`; merges parent entities when branch has < 5 own entities
- `app/src/server/graph/queries.ts` — `listConversationEntities` similarly extended with inherited entity support
- `app/src/server/chat/chat-processor.ts` — Detects branch via `branched_from` query, passes `inheritedEntityIds` to context loaders and orchestrator
- `app/src/server/chat/context.ts` — `buildChatContext` threads `inheritedEntityIds` to `listConversationEntities`
- `app/src/server/chat/handler.ts` — `runOrchestrator` passes `inheritedEntityIds` to `buildChatContext`

**UI:**
- `app/src/client/routes/chat-page.tsx` — Branch button in chat header, `onBranchConversation()` handler, recursive `renderConversationItem()` for nested sidebar display with `↳` prefix and indentation

**Tests:** All 129 unit tests pass (0 failures).
